title: Lean Africa-site pregnancy-related RCTs as a transfer template
summary: An E156 micro-paper and scaffold summarizing how often Africa-site pregnancy-related RCTs meet a pragmatic shortlist for cheaper and faster delivery.
primary_estimand: Proportion of Africa-site trials meeting the transfer shortlist
data: Local topic CSV files in the data/ directory
source_article: Derived from local ClinicalTrials.gov topic benchmark outputs
body: In Africa-site pregnancy-related randomized trials registered on ClinicalTrials.gov, how often do studies meet a practical shortlist for cheaper and faster delivery? Eighty completed, results-posted trials formed the Africa benchmark, and twelve met pre-specified transfer shortlist criteria on enrollment, sites, countries, and prospective registration. We performed a registry-based descriptive scan, compared Africa-site records with a global benchmark, and treated the shortlist proportion as the primary estimand with Wilson intervals. The shortlist proportion was 0.15, with 95% confidence interval 0.09 to 0.24. Median results-reporting lag was 529.5 days, interquartile range 415.2 to 865.2, while South Africa, Kenya, Uganda, and Egypt dominated the Africa-site footprint. These findings suggest pregnancy-related behavioral and lower-footprint designs may be adaptable overall in African settings, though the domain looks less transfer-rich than malaria for maternal programs with strong antenatal follow-up. Interpretation is limited by registry proxies rather than budgets, sparse result-linked publications, and use of pregnancy as a practical search term for maternal health.

primary_plot.caption: No numeric forest-style rows embedded for this descriptive registry scan.
